mainmatter.p: Let \(B\) be a category. Define the displayed category \(\overline{B}\) over \(B\) as follows:

ol.li: For \(x\in B\), define \(\overline{B}_{x}\) as the collection of pairs \((\bar{x}\in B,p_{x}:x\to\bar{x})\).
For \(f : x\to y\in B\), define \(\overline{B}_{f}\) to be the collection of commuting squares in the following configuration:

mainmatter.p: Dually to the fundamental self-indexing, every category \(B\) can also be displayed over itself via its coslices \(B^{\setminus x}\).
Let \(B\) be a category. Define the displayed category \(\overline{B}\) over \(B\) as follows:

ol.li: For \(x\in B\), define \(\overline{B}_{x}\) as the collection of pairs \((\bar{x}\in B,p_{x}:x\to\bar{x})\).
For \(f : x\to y\in B\), define \(\overline{B}_{f}\) to be the collection of commuting squares in the following configuration:

mainmatter.p: Prove that \(\overline{B}\) is a cocartesian fibration if and only if \(B\) has pushouts.
Prove that the total category of \(\overline{B}\) is the arrow category \(B^{\to}\), and the projection is the domain functor.
Prove that \(\overline{B}\) is a cartesian fibration for any category \(B\).
